typecheck.command: xcodebuild build-for-testing -project OpenBurnBar.xcodeproj -scheme OpenBurnBar -destination "platform=macOS,arch=arm64" -clonedSourcePackagesDirPath .spm-cache -derivedDataPath .derived-data/ci-typecheck CODE_SIGNING_ALLOWED=NO CODE_SIGNING_REQUIRED=NO

blockingIssues.description: Gap repair inspects only the first 1000 conversation documents per sweep (no pagination), so stale records outside that slice may never be reconciled.
Gap repair skips missing-source conversations instead of purging stale search artifacts, so missed delete events can leave stale indexed data.

Projection still calls replace-all chunk persistence paths (`replaceSearchChunks` / `replaceChunks`) that rewrite full document chunk sets, so unchanged chunks are not actually skipped at persistence/index level.
New offset pagination queries order only by non-unique timestamp expressions; without deterministic tie-breakers, page traversal can skip or duplicate rows.

appliedUpdates.description: Updated .factory/library/architecture.md with projection sweep test semantics, chunk identity source-version coupling, and deterministic tie-break requirements for offset pagination.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Enforce required startup reads and truthful procedure reporting: if `.factory/library/user-testing.md` is required, transcript evidence should be mandatory before `followedProcedure=true` is accepted.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Two reviews (m3-indexing-reliability-recovery-and-requeue and m3-cross-surface-upgrade-and-refresh-efficiency) found no `user-testing.md` read in transcript skeletons while handoffs reported procedure followed.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify allowed deviation handling when strict TDD-first and retrieval-eval steps are not practical (for example flaky deterministic tests): require explicit deviation notes and alternative evidence rather than silent omission.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: m3-event-driven-indexing-and-gap-repair-core review observed missing red/green and retrieval-eval evidence despite skill requiring both for projection changes.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Require concrete write-amplification/skip counters in verification output when feature scope claims incremental indexing efficiency gains.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: m3-chunk-diff-and-embedding-efficiency-guards review noted skill-level counter evidence was absent from handoff despite explicit requirement.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Standardize worker handoff schema expectations for `skillFeedback` presence so scrutiny reviewers can audit procedure compliance consistently.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: m3-event-driven-indexing-and-gap-repair-core handoff omitted `skillFeedback`, while other milestone handoffs included it.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Align VAL-INDEX-004/005 wording with current chunk identity design (chunk IDs include sourceVersionID) or explicitly require a chunk ID model redesign for untouched-ID preservation semantics.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: m3-chunk-diff-and-embedding-efficiency-guards review found contract/test expectations around untouched chunk persistence conflict with source-version-coupled chunk identity behavior.
